rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in the appropriate AADL scope: component type annexes for interface behavior, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use assign statements in implementation scope only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by placing contracts in valid scopes without redesigning component structure.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents in component type contracts.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Implementation-level bindings must be in implementation scope after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for implementation-level bindings only.
Do not reference event ports as numeric or Boolean values without explicit data representation.
